title: Critique engine (critic-deep.ts) + opt-in threading
description: Build src/fleet/critic-deep.ts (the bounded fresh-critic critique/refine loop) plus the additive opt-in threading in src/fleet/decomposer-deep.ts so decomposeGoalDeep({...,critique:true}) routes a structurally-valid baseline manifest through runCritiqueLoop before returning. Fully testable end-to-end via a ScriptedClient fake; NO CLI in this sprint.
estimatedFiles: src/fleet/decomposer-deep.ts (additive edits: DecomposeDeepInput.critique?, runExpandStage.critiqueFeedback?, decomposeGoalDeep routing + capture outline once)

successCriteria.description: npm run build (tsc) is clean after adding critic-deep.ts and the decomposer-deep.ts edits.

Strict typecheck passes, no explicit any; critic-deep.ts imports ONLY LLMClient/Message types from ../providers/types.js (no SDK types) and uses import type; ESM .js extensions on all relative imports.
ESLint is clean on critic-deep.ts + critic-deep.test.ts (consistent-type-imports, no-unused, ESM .js).

Constants test asserts CRITIQUE_MAX_ROUNDS===1, CRITIQUE_PARSE_MAX_RETRIES===1, DEEP_CRITIQUE_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS===8, and DEEP_CRITIQUE_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS===DEEP_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS+CRITIQUE_MAX_ROUNDS*((1+CRITIQUE_PARSE_MAX_RETRIES)+(1+DEEP_EXPAND_MAX_RETRIES)) (audit relation).

validateVerdict NEVER throws: returns {ok:false,error} for empty/non-JSON text, bad verdict enum, and missing feedback; returns {ok:true,verdict} for a valid {verdict,feedback}. Mirrors validateOutline's discriminated-union return.

Both critic chat calls captured by ScriptedClient have jsonObjectMode===true AND responseSchema===undefined; callCritic uses its own CRITIQUE_SYSTEM_PROMPT and a fresh message array presenting the candidate as third-party input (not an appended EXPAND turn). getCriticVerdict fail-open: after (1+CRITIQUE_PARSE_MAX_RETRIES)=2 unparseable responses it returns {verdict:'approve',feedback:''} without throwing; the coercion retry uses the 3-message [user,assistant,user] shape.

runCritiqueLoop: scripted reject-then-richer-expand folds critic feedback into a FRESH runExpandStage (critiqueFeedback present in the re-expand call) and returns the re-expanded multi-child manifest; scripted all-reject returns accept-best (tiebreak most children, else first-seen baseline) and NEVER throws; total ScriptedClient.chat calls across a fully-failing critique run never exceed DEEP_CRITIQUE_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS=8.

decomposeGoalDeep({...,critique:true}) on a degenerate baseline (2 children) with scripted reject + richer re-expand returns the multi-child manifest, and the manifest passed to the critic is already FleetManifestSchema-valid (critic after structural gate). decomposeGoalDeep with critique absent/false emits ZERO critic calls and a chat sequence byte-identical to Phase 3 (plan+expand only, <=4 calls); decomposer.ts/manifest.ts/providers stay byte-unchanged.

generatorNotes: Read .bober/architecture/arch-20260618-fleet-expand-deep-critique-architecture.md + adr-1..5 FIRST — the Component Breakdown gives every TS signature and the Integration Strategy gives the exact call chain and gate ordering. Mirror, do not invent: validateVerdict mirrors validateOutline (decomposer-deep.ts:107-155) — same direct-parse -> ```json fence -> first-brace fallback -> Zod safeParse ladder, only the schema differs (CritiqueVerdictSchema). callCritic mirrors callExpand (decomposer-deep.ts:202-239) for the jsonObjectMode:true call + 3-message [user,assistant,user] coercion, BUT builds its OWN clean CRITIQUE_SYSTEM_PROMPT and never references the EXPAND conversation or tells the model it authored the manifest. Re-expand reuses runExpandStage (decomposer-deep.ts:280-315) by adding an optional critiqueFeedback?:string appended to the FIRST EXPAND user message only when present — do NOT fork runExpandStage. In decomposeGoalDeep, capture the Outline from runPlanStage ONCE and pass it to the baseline expand AND runCritiqueLoop (no 2nd PLAN call). Route to runCritiqueLoop ONLY when input.critique===true; otherwise the existing plan->expand->return path must be byte-unchanged. NEVER set responseSchema anywhere. Constants block + co-located audit test mirror decomposer-deep.ts:71-74 and decomposer-deep.test.ts:76-80. Tests use the ScriptedClient fake from decomposer-deep.test.ts (records every ChatParams, returns scripted responses). Commit only critic-deep.ts, critic-deep.test.ts, and decomposer-deep.ts with message 'bober(sprint-1): critique engine (critic-deep.ts) + opt-in threading'. Do NOT touch index.ts this sprint.
evaluatorNotes: Independently verify, do not trust the report: (1) grep responseSchema in critic-deep.ts must be EMPTY; both critic calls assert jsonObjectMode===true && responseSchema===undefined. (2) The audit relation DEEP_CRITIQUE_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS===8 must be a real test assertion, not a comment. (3) The all-reject ceiling test must assert ScriptedClient.calls.length<=8 AND that the loop resolves (never throws). (4) Fail-open: 2 unparseable critic responses -> verdict approve, no throw. (5) Byte-identity: decomposeGoalDeep with critique absent must emit the SAME chat call count/order as a Phase-3 scripted run (<=4, zero critic calls) — run the existing decomposer-deep.test.ts to confirm no regression. (6) `git diff` must show only critic-deep.ts (new), critic-deep.test.ts (new), decomposer-deep.ts (additive — assert zero deleted lines via `git diff | grep '^-[^-]'` empty); decomposer.ts, manifest.ts, index.ts, providers/ NOT in the diff. (7) Full npm test green. Required strategies: build + typecheck (required), lint + unit-test.
